title: Elatinoides cirrhosa
intro: Elatinoides cirrhosa, also known as Indian valerian, is a plant species of the family Elatinaceae, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, as well as its ability to improve sleep quality and reduce stress levels. The bioactive constituents of Elatinoides cirrhosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Elatinoides cirrhosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae, commonly known as the water-dottocks or water-dyllis. The family Haloragaceae is a group of aquatic plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. These plants are often found in wetlands and are characterized by their distinctive leaf and flower structures. The genus Elatinoides is a small group of aquatic plants that are closely related to the genus Elatine. The classification of Elatinoides cirrhosa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
